by an inner or spiritual conflict. As in the case of Luther, Marx
too was opposed to superficial religion, outward pomp and
extravagance and vanity. He carried this to the extremes.

94 Rectification of Birth Time
There is a planet here in the sixth, cut off from the rest. This
indicates the failure on the part of Marx to adjust himself to
those around him, including Engels. This western hemisphere
gets a particular emphasis from Mars who is also at the
beginning of his debilitation. This has made him an uncon
scious pioneer in human history, not on the spiritual side but
on the side governing the affairs of the sixth, viz. servants,
labourers and workers. Mars has given him a unique place in
the tragic history of man. Sigmund Freud has all his planets
from the ninth to the twelfth, with Mars alone in the third.
The southern hemisphere has made him purely selfless to the
extent that he fought against odds defending his theories of
